title_original: US-Israel war on Iran: Indian homes stockpile electric stoves to beat gas crunch
content_original: Indian households are rushing to buy electric induction stoves, draining ⁠stocks online and in stores, ⁠amid fears of a potential cooking gas ⁠shortage tied to the Middle East conflict. India, the world’s second-largest importer of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG), has invoked emergency powers to boost supplies for households even as availability tightens for commercial users including canteens, hostels and restaurants. Meanwhile, consumers are buying electric cooking appliances as a precaution,...
title_en: Indian households stockpile electric stoves amid fears of gas shortage linked to Middle East conflict
summary_en: Indian households are purchasing electric induction stoves in anticipation of a potential liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) shortage caused by the ongoing Iran–United States conflict in the Middle East. As the world's second-largest LPG importer, India has invoked emergency powers to secure supplies for residential use while commercial availability tightens due to regional hostilities.
